name: Separate Construction from Use
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 11: Systems
problem: Object construction is mixed with business logic across the codebase, making it hard to change the wiring of the application and impossible to substitute test doubles without modifying production code.
steps: Move all object construction and wiring to a single startup point such as main() or a dedicated factory/builder.
Never let application business-logic code call 'new' on collaborators it depends on.
Pass fully constructed objects into the application code through constructors or setters (Dependency Injection).
Use the Abstract Factory pattern when the application must create objects at runtime but still wants to keep construction details out of business logic.
Alternatively use a DI framework (Spring, Guice) to manage the construction phase.
